title: Commercial Driving Apprentice
description: **About ATC** ATC is a global logistics, white\-glove, and technical services specialist for data centers. For over 45 years, we've helped deliver Europe's data centers safely, securely, and right the first time. Our work includes specialist IT transport and relocations, as well as full data center commissioning and decommissioning. As part of the Arvato Group, we benefit from the strength, scale, and global expertise of one of the world's leading supply chain and technology partners. What truly sets us apart, however, is our people. We invest in training, development, and continuous improvement, and we take pride in building long\-term relationships with our clients and teams across Europ **e.** **The Role:** The Commercial Driver Apprentice (CDA) will join our Transport Operations team, supporting delivery routes across Ireland, the UK and Europe. This 2‑year apprenticeship, delivered with ATU Sligo and industry partners, includes one college day per week during semesters and leads to a QQI Level 6 Higher Certificate in Business in Transport Services. The successful candidate will be trained in and receive both the C and CE license and to perform all tasks efficiently and to a high standard, following established procedures. Once trained, they will carry out deliveries professionally and take full responsibility for the vehicle and its load. The CDA will rotate through the transport and technical services teams. **Responsibilities:** * Arrive on client sites fully prepared with the required vehicle, equipment, uniform, documentation and PPE, following agreed schedules, defined routes and time plans. * Safely operate, prepare, inspect, load and unload delivery vehicles and IT hardware, reporting any mechanical, safety or scheduling issues to the Operations Manager without delay. * Follow all assigned driving routes and time schedules, notifying planners, managers or supervisors immediately of delays, route changes or safety concerns. * Unpack, position and install IT hardware such as server racks, safely lift 30\-40kg regularly (and up to 50kg occasionally), and complete fibre and copper cabling to required standards. * Complete all digital and manual documentation accurately—including delivery logs, warehouse, haulage, distribution, shipping and daily checklists—and update records promptly if job orders change. * Maintain high levels of customer service and professional conduct at all times, adhering to uniform, performance, health, safety, security, and client procedures. * Perform daily administrative tasks, ensuring all documentation is kept up to date, stored correctly, and shared appropriately with relevant teams. * Comply with all regulations relating to driving and duty performance, including tachograph requirements, and participate in ongoing education, skills development and internal driving practice sessions. * Carry out additional duties aligned with your skills and competencies to support safe, efficient, and high\-quality operational delivery. **Requirements** **About you:** * Full Category B license, 18\+, and medically fit to drive. * Meet one of the following: * Leaving Certificate with required English and Math's grades * QQI Level 5/6 in a related field * Mature applicant (23\+) with 2\+ years' commercial driving experience, employer recommendation, and suitable skills * Flexible, resilient, able to work under pressure and meet deadlines. * Able to commute or relocate to Dublin before starting. * Willing to learn vehicle maintenance, new routes, and work closely with Planning. * Strong customer service focus and ability to work in a fast‑paced, white‑glove service environment. * Results‑driven with a can‑do attitude and strong communication skills * Advantageous: Knowledge of a European language. * 'Proficiency in English Language necessary as all course materials are delivered through English' **Benefits** * AIG Virtual Care Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Company pension plan (subject to meeting specific qualifying criteria) * Opportunity to work from abroad 30 days per year * Opportunities for continuing professional development

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - South African Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Start Date: August 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give graduates exposure to both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* **Who are we looking for?** * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-orientated and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view **What’s in it for you?** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal employee groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1\-1\-1 model **Map Your Future** With our Graduate Program you will have the choice of career paths * You can choose to take a Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * You can choose to take a Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing **Salesforce in Dublin** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. **Accommodations** If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. **Posting Statement** At Salesforce we believe that the business of business is to improve the state of our world. Each of us has a responsibility to drive Equality in our communities and workplaces. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects society through inclusive programs and initiatives such as equal pay, employee resource groups, inclusive benefits, and more. Learn more about Equality at Salesforce and explore our benefits. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org are Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employers. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org do not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org will not pay any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Salesforce.com or Salesforce.org. Salesforce welcomes all. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Consulting Associate - 2026
description: **Introduction** A career in IBM Consulting is built on long\-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long\-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences. **Your Role And Responsibilities** As a Graduate Consultant at IBM, you will embark on an exciting career journey as you build strong technical, sales, solutioning and project delivery skills, within a consulting business. You will become familiar with IBM’s organisation and ow we work with clients to address their most complex business and technology challenges. Collaboration with leading experts from across IBM and our partner ecosystem provides hands‑on exposure to real project challenges and best‑practice sales, solution design and delivery. In this environment you will develop a clear understanding of how technologies including AI, automation, security, cloud, business application and data analytics solutions are delivered, and how they create measurable business value. Working in our Global Services Integration Hub (SIH) in Dublin, you will deepen your understanding of how modern technologies are architected, delivered and scaled across our global organisation. You will gain experience across industries, applications, enterprise platforms and solution domains, preparing you for future roles in business/technical solution design and delivery, project management and leadership, and establishing a strong foundation for long‑term career growth. The role provides a clear pathway for career progression. Typical progression routes include Business Analyst, Generative AI Specialist, Project Manager, IT Architect, Technology Specialist and Security Specialist, Sales and Strategy. **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** IBM Graduate Consultant, you will gain experience across areas such as Artificial Intelligence, Advanced Analytics, Cognitive Solutions, Enterprise Applications, Cloud Services and Security. **We Require You To** * As an equal opportunities employer, we welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship now or in the future. This role requires regular travel to clients across Ireland, the UK, and Europe, so candidates must have the legal right to work and travel within these regions without restriction. * This role requires flexibility in working patterns, including up to five days per week on\-site, at our Dublin office on the Technology Campus, Damastown and being available from time to time for Global Teams meetings. * Be on track for, or have achieved, a 2:1 grade in a Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics focused discipline. Graduates from other fields are welcome with a demonstrated interest in technology and AI * Think logically and analytically, approach problems with structure and curiosity. * Communicate clearly and confidently with technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Be motivated to understand how modern technology—including AI—solves real business challenges. * Work effectively and proactively in teams, demonstrate a positive attitude, and thrive in collaborative environments. * Have some experience in programming, data tools or an understanding of basic IT concepts. * Demonstrate awareness of cybersecurity principles and good technology practices. * Available to commence full\-time employment with expected start dates in September, October or November.

title: Contract Administrator (Intermediate) - Infrastructure
description: Turner \& Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. We are majority\-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner \& Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. Please visit our website: www.turnerandtownsend.com **Job Description** The Intermediate Contract Administrator is responsible for supporting with the administration of contracts during the construction phase and at the outset of the operational phases. The role holder oversees the administration of the Consultancy and Advisor Contracts. The Intermediate Contract Administrator is required to manage the contract manual, processes, and procedures in relation to changes, claims, organising meetings, communication, and ensuring that all issues are resolved or brought to the attention of the relevant parties. The role holder is required to organise filing and documentation and oversee the operation execution of financial management processes. * Identify and implement the appropriate contract administration systems (CEMAR or similar) during the procurement process. * Oversee the administration of Contracts during both the construction and operational phases, ensuring compliance with contract terms and conditions. * Manage the administration of contracts related to consultants and advisors, ensuring their obligations are met and services are delivered as agreed. * Handle change and claims processes, including evaluating, negotiating, and resolving claims in accordance with established procedures. * Organise and facilitate meetings with stakeholders, ensuring effective communication and collaboration between all parties involved in the project. * Identify and resolve issues promptly or escalate them to relevant parties to ensure smooth project execution. * Maintain accurate and organise filing systems for all project\-related documentation to ensure easy access and retrieval. * Oversee the financial aspects of contract management, including budgeting, cost control, and financial reporting, to ensure responsibility is taken. * Develop, implement, and manage contract manuals, processes, and procedures, ensuring consistency and efficiency in contract administration activities. * Engage with stakeholders to understand their needs and expectations and ensure the contract administration aligns with strategic goals. * Monitor compliance with contractual obligations and prepare regular reports on contract status, performance, and issues. * Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with the construction and operational phases. * Perform additional tasks, activities or other duties as may be required or assigned as part of the role. **Experience** * 5 years' experience in document control and a proven track record of handling project document control * activities in high value metro rail projects or projects of a similar scale / complexity. * Knowledge of quality management and process improvement practices and systems, preferably within * the construction sector. * Strong experience in document control activities relating to compliance, quality and policies. * Strong experience working with document management systems, databases and software. **Skills** * Proficiency in document management systems (DMS), databases, and software such as Microsoft Office, SharePoint, or specialised document control software. * Strong knowledge of project management principles and practices to align document control activities with project goals. * Strong knowledge of industry standards, regulations, and legal requirements related to document handling and storage. * Strong understanding data protection principles to safeguard sensitive and confidential information. * Ability to adapt to changing project needs and document management technologies or processes. * Ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills to facilitate clear and consistent dialogue between all parties involved. * Ability to govern and drive outcomes to ensure high quality service. **Qualifications** * Minimum Level 8 degree (or equivalent) in relevant subject area e.g., Project Management, Business Administration etc. Advantageous however not mandatory for this role: * Project Management Professional (PMP) * Certified Document Controller (CDC) **Additional Information** **What we offer you:** * Full time * Competitive remuneration and attractive range of benefits * 8% Pension * 23 days Annual leave (\+ 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and collaborative colleagues Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance. Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community. Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn *It is strictly against Turner \& Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner \& Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.* *Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner \& Townsend personal e\-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner \& Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner \& Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review.*

title: Customer Assistant Phoneshop (Permanent 30 - 35 Hours) - Tyrrelstown
description: **About the role** We are looking for Tesco Phoneshop Team Members in this store. Every part of Tesco Mobile puts our customers first – our tariffs, our shops, and our award\-winning Customer Care. We’ve teamed up with 3 giving our savvy customers 99% coverage. In our stores, our colleagues aren’t on commission. It’s all about finding our customers the perfect phone and tariff. * Are you warm, friendly, and approachable? * Do you have the drive and motivation to work in a sales and service environment? * Can you adapt to working in a fast\-paced ever\-changing environment? * Do you enjoy working with the latest technology? * Are you a team player who supports others and builds great team spirit? * Do you enjoy building relationships with customers to establish their needs and help them choose the right products for them? **Should you be successful in your application, your offer will be subject to and conditional upon you providing your bank account details before your agreed start date.** **You will be responsible for** * Always put the customer first and consider them in everything you do. * Get to know your customers and serve them with pride. * Help to ensure products and services are available for customers at all times. * Keep the shop floor and back areas clean and tidy at all times. * Using the training you receive, follow department routines and processes. * Follow all company policies and adhere to Health and Safety routines. **You will need** * A passion for delivering great service, greeting customers with a smile and serving them with pride * The ability to build rapport with customers, meaning they leave the store having experienced a great phoneshop shopping trip * To take the initiative and make decisions that are right for our customers * Work well within a team and communicate effectively and openly with others * Build relationships with colleagues to create a team spirit, having fun and celebrating success * Be at work on time, well presented and ready to be a Tesco Mobile brand ambassador **Whats in it for you?** We offer excellent benefits that help make Tesco a great place to work. These include but aren’t limited to: * Competitive Salary * Bonus Scheme\* * Our Virtual GP service provides free and confidential access to an online GP through video GP consultations, and online prescription service for colleagues. The service is also available to families of colleagues who live at the same address for a reduced cost. * 24/7 access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and family members for in the moment emotional support along with free access to a range of wellbeing services supporting you under our new Wellbeing First approach of Body, Mind and Life. * Colleague Clubcard offering 10% discount across stores and online, with 15% discount weekends once a month (Friday to Sunday), and 20% off twice a year. There is also 20% off F\&F clothing, all year long, these various discounts can be extended to a family member with a 2 nd Clubcard available.\* * Life Assurance * Pension Scheme\* * Save As You Earn Scheme\* * Paid Family Leave * Bike to Work * Long Service Awards * Commuter Scheme \*Entry requirements and rules of the scheme apply **About Us** Our vision at Tesco is to become every customer’s favourite way to shop, whether they are at home or out on the move. Our core purpose is “Serving our customers, communities and planet a little better every day”. Serving means more than a transactional relationship with our customers. It means acting as a responsible and sustainable business for all stakeholders, for the communities we are part of, and for the planet. Through initiatives like the Tesco Community Fund, our association with Children’s Health Foundation, our partnership with FoodCloud and our Stronger Starts programme, we have supported over 21,000 community groups, raised over €7\.5 million for medical equipment, and provided almost 15 million meals nationwide, to those living in food insecurity. Tesco has operated in the Irish retail market since 1997, and with 170 stores nationwide, we employ over 13,000 people in cities and towns around Ireland, supporting almost 45,000 jobs directly and indirectly. We partner with over 500 Irish suppliers \- of which almost three\-quarters are small and medium enterprises \- which in turn supports almost 13,000 farming families around Ireland. Tesco is the single largest buyer of Irish food and drink in the world, buying €1\.6 Billion a year; more than any other single country in the European Union, more than even the USA which you perceive as a massive buyer of Irish food. We work closely with suppliers across Ireland who are dedicated to making the best products for our customers and this is reflected in the numerous awards our products have won over the years including Blás Na hÉireann, World Steak Challenge and Great Taste Awards. We are proud to have an inclusive culture at Tesco where everyone truly feels able to be themselves. It is embedded in our values: we treat people how they want to be treated. At Tesco, we not only celebrate diversity, but recognise the value and opportunity it brings. We always want our colleagues to feel they can be themselves at work and we are committed to helping them be at their best. We know life looks a little different for each of us. That’s why at Tesco, we always welcome chats about flexible working. Some people are at the start of their careers, some want the freedom to do the things they love. Others are going through life\-changing moments like becoming a carer, nearing retirement, adapting to parenthood, or something else. So, talk to us throughout your application about how we can support. Tesco is committed to celebrating diversity and everyone is welcome at Tesco. We're committed to providing a fully inclusive recruitment process, allowing candidates the opportunity to thrive and inform us of any reasonable adjustments they may require. For further information on the accessibility support we can offer, please click http://roi.tesco\-careers.com/accessibility/ **How can I let Tesco know I need additional support?** Please let us know you need additional support by emailing **tescoireland.recruitment@tesco.ie** This will allow us to understand more about you and how we can best support you through the recruitment process.

title: Customer Service Representative II
description: **About Us** Stericycle is a U.S. based business\-to\-business services company and leading provider of compliance\-based solutions that protects people and brands, promotes health and well\-being, and safeguards the environment. Since our founding over 30 years ago, we have grown from a small start\-up in medical waste management into a leader across a range of increasingly complex and highly regulated arenas, serving healthcare organizations and commercial businesses of every size. Every day, we help our customers solve complex challenges by safely managing materials that could otherwise spread disease, contaminate the environment, or compromise one’s identity. Join us on our mission to protect health and well\-being in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way. **Position Purpose** As part of a busy transport team, our Customer Service Representatives (Class C drivers) are responsible for the collection, destruction, movement and unloading of confidential waste paper and electronic storage devices on behalf of various customers within the professional, financial and retail industries. **Key Job Activities** Working a 40\-hour week over 4 days and acting as a key point of customer contact, you will also be expected to reconcile/complete all relevant documentation and issue a certificate of destruction via a hand\-held terminal. Remove the console bags from each console and tie them tightly so no paper escapes while transferring to the truck. Ensure that no material is left in the console. Replace the console bag with an empty bag and lock the console. Take material directly to the shredder truck and proceed to shred. Make sure the truck gates are secure so that none of the contents escape from the truck during the shred. Immediately upon completion of the shredding locate the client's for signature. Give and explain certificate of destruction to client. Record time shred was completed. **Education** Junior and Leaving Certificates. **Experience (EMEAA)** Relevant driving experience and a Class C licence, along with a full driver CPC card. The ability to undertake heavy lifting and physically demanding work, involving the safe movement of large wheeled trolleys on and off vehicles. Excellent customer service skills and plenty of flexibility around working hours. Ability to read and understand client information sheets. Ability to write clear messages on daily reporting sheets. **Benefits** **Certifications and/or Licenses:** **Stericycle Offers You** * Contributory Pension Scheme * Life Insurance * Access to SteriCares, our employee support fund * Stericycle University – Our online library of self\-development \& learning * Annual performance related pay review. * Referral Scheme (Earn by introducing people in your network to the Stericycle family) * Flu voucher * Eye Test voucher And more… **Disclaimer** The above description is meant to provide a summary of the nature and level of work being performed; it should not be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and requirements of the job. This document does not create an employment contract, implied or otherwise, and it does not constitute any right or guarantee of employment condition. This position is open to people with disabilities. Stericycle will consider requests for workplace accommodations for protected physical or mental limitations in accordance with its human resources and risks prevention policies and local laws. To the extent permissible under local law, and consistent with business necessity, Stericycle reserves the right to modify the content formally or informally, either verbally or in writing, at any time with or without advance notice.

title: Customer Service Specialist
description: **Position** : Customer Care Representative (Motor Fleet Industry) **Salary** : 32,000 to 36,000 \+ 5,000 yearly bonus guaranteed **Location** : Sandyford, Leopardstown (3/4 days on site 1/2 day WFH) As our clients team continues to expand alongside the rollout of our new VOR system, they are seeking a customer\-focused professional to join the Operations Team. This role will serve as the primary point of contact for drivers and customers, supporting booking management, handling queries, and ensuring smooth day\-to\-day operations. This is an excellent opportunity for an individual with experience in customer service, call centre operations, or administration who thrives in a fast\-paced environment, enjoys problem\-solving, and is committed to delivering a high standard of service. ***They sound like an amazing brand, I would love to work for them! What will I be doing every day?*** * Acting as a key point of contact for driver and customer queries via phone and email * Coordinating and scheduling services, repairs, and appointments * Providing customers with regular updates on progress and service status * Liaising with garages, suppliers, and third\-party providers to ensure efficient service delivery * Maintaining accurate internal systems, records, and operational documentation * Supporting the Operations Team to ensure vehicles and services run efficiently and effectively * Resolving issues and queries promptly, professionally, and with a customer\-focused approach ***Sounds great, what kind of person are they looking for?*** * Previous experience in a customer service, call centre, retail, administration, or similar customer\-facing role * Strong communication skills with confidence handling phone calls, emails, and internal systems * Excellent organisational skills with strong attention to detail * Ability to manage and prioritise multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment * A positive, proactive team player with a flexible and can\-do attitude * Competent IT skills, including experience using Outlook, Excel, and internal systems * Previous experience within the automotive, fleet, or service industry(not essential) * Experience coordinating bookings and liaising with suppliers or external service providers

title: Customer Support Technician
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. **What You’ll Do** As a Customer Support Technician, you'll work hands\-on in a cleanroom environment to build and test our multimillion\-dollar display and wafer fabrication equipment that’s behind the technology we use every day— like the VR headset you use for gaming, the smart watch that tracks your heart rate, and advancing generations of your smart phone. As key member of a team of highly skilled engineers, technicians, and problem\-solvers, you’ll receive strong collaborative support and contribute your new ideas to help us continuously improve our processes and business. **This role is based at our customer site in Leixlip, Co. Kildare and operates on a rotating day‑and‑night shift pattern. Full training will be provided.** **Role Responsibilities:** * Assist in planning the daily work schedule within assigned territory to ensure completion of Installation, Preventive Maintenance and/or remedial maintenance. * Use escalation policies to keep management informed of down situations. * Verify and approve the operational quality of system equipment. Perform quality checks daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, biannual, and annual periodic maintenance on systems within established time frames. Also perform wet cleans if/as required. * Instruct customers and trades personnel in the installation, operation and maintenance of systems. Troubleshoot and correct process variations on systems partially with assistance from senior engineers. * Assume responsibility for complete customer satisfaction. Assist in planning, communicating, and co\-ordinating support plans with customer management. * Perform start\-up, warranty, paid service, and service contract activities. Perform TIER 0,I \& II start\-ups within established time frames. * Use training and experience to identify some process related system problems. * Develop and implement plans to increase productivity. Tracks equipment performance, install cycle times and plan uptime improvement * Plan and execute support activities on a wide range of Applied Materials' systems. **Minimum Qualifications:** * An associate degree and field experience or technical certification * 1–2 years of related work experience in customer/field support * A willingness to learn how to read and interpret electrical and mechanical schematics * Clear and effective written/verbal communications skills, with an emphasis on documenting details * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office suite (Excel, Word, and PowerPoint) * Basic mechanical aptitude and knowledge in any of the following: pneumatics, hydraulics, electronics, vacuum, or thermodynamics * Knowledge of hand tools and proper usage, including a digital multimeter **Physical Requirements:** This is a physically demanding position that often requires standing for extended periods of time, walking, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, lifting, the ability to distinguish between colors, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, the use of hand and power tools, and driving or operating heavy machinery including forklifts. Additionally, because this position may involve working in a clean room, the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields may be required. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 10% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: We are seeking a **GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) Analyst** to join our client’s security team in Dublin. This role will be crucial in a fast\-paced environment where you'll be responsible for ensuring our security posture aligns with key regulatory frameworks and industry best practices. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in **ISO 27001** , **NIST** , and **DORA** . **Responsibilities** * **Compliance \& Governance:** Lead the implementation and maintenance of security frameworks, specifically **ISO 27001** , **NIST CSF** , and the new **DORA** regulation. * **Risk Management:** Conduct detailed risk assessments and gap analyses to identify vulnerabilities and compliance gaps against specified frameworks. * **Audits:** Coordinate and support internal and external audits, including preparing evidence and documentation for auditors. You will be the point of contact for audit inquiries. * **Policy \& Procedure:** Develop, update, and manage security policies, standards, and procedures to ensure they align with the latest regulatory requirements. * **Reporting:** Create and present clear, actionable reports on our compliance status, risk posture, and remediation efforts to both technical teams and senior leadership. * **Remediation:** Track and facilitate the remediation of identified control gaps, collaborating with various teams to ensure timely resolution. **Skills \& Qualifications** * **Experience:** At least 3\-5 years of experience in a GRC, information security, or IT audit role. * **Technical Expertise:** A comprehensive and practical understanding of **ISO 27001** and its implementation. Experience with **NIST frameworks** (e.g., CSF, SP 800 series) is essential. * **DORA Knowledge:** A strong understanding of the **Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)** is a key requirement. You must know what it entails and how to apply it within a financial or IT services organization. * **Certifications (Preferred):** Relevant professional certifications are highly desirable, such as **ISO 27001 Lead Implementer/Auditor** , **CRISC** , **CISA** , or **CISSP** . * **Communication:** Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. You must be able to translate complex security and compliance requirements into a business context for non\-technical audiences. * **Problem\-Solving:** Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities, with a meticulous attention to detail.

title: Data Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Data Engineer **Job type:** Contract **Job Location:** Dublin/Hybrid **Company Overview:** A global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes, and unlock growth with speed. The company harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world's leading corporations in industries such as insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media, and retail, among others. Founded in 1999, the company operates with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity, and respect. **The Role:** Our client is seeking a **hands\-on Databricks Data Engineer** to support a high\-impact data transformation initiative focused on automating reporting and dashboard processes. You will work with structured and unstructured data sources, **building and maintaining scalable data ingestion and transformation pipelines** within a Databricks medallion architecture. This role is delivery\-focused, working closely with Data Architects and senior stakeholders to implement data solutions, develop pipelines across Bronze / Silver / Gold layers, and ensure data quality, performance, and reliability within a modern cloud\-based data platform. **Key Responsibilities:** * Data Pipeline Development: Design, build, and maintain data ingestion and transformation pipelines using Databricks, working across structured and unstructured data sources. * Medallion Architecture Implementation: Develop and support data pipelines across bronze, silver and gold layers, ensuring data is cleansed, transformed, and optimised for downstream reporting and analytics. * Data Integration \& Processing: Implement batch and streaming data pipelines using tools such as Databricks, Spark, and Azure Data Factory (ADF) to enable efficient data movement and processing. * Collaboration with Architects \& Stakeholders: Work closely with Data Architects and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into reliable, maintainable data engineering solutions. * Data Quality \& Validation: Support the implementation of data quality checks, validation rules, and monitoring processes to ensure accuracy and reliability of data pipelines. * Performance Optimisation: Assist in optimising data pipeline performance, scalability, and cost efficiency within Databricks and cloud environments. * Data Governance \& Standards: Follow established best practices for data governance, lineage, and metadata management, ensuring compliance with organisational standards. * Testing, Deployment \& Release Support: Support testing, deployment, and release processes, ensuring solutions are robust and production ready. * Documentation: Create and maintain technical documentation, including pipeline logic, data flows, and transformation rules. * Continuous Improvement: Stay up to date with Databricks features and modern data engineering practices, contributing to ongoing improvements in tooling, frameworks, and ways of working. **Experience Required:** * Education \& Experience: Bachelor's degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field, with 3\-6 years' experience in data engineering, data warehousing, or related roles. * Databricks \& Data Engineering Skills: Hands\-on experience with Databricks and Spark\-based data processing, including building and maintaining data pipelines. * Data Integration \& Processing: Experience working with ETL/ELT pipelines using tools such as Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), or similar frameworks. * Data Platform \& Database Knowledge: Experience working with enterprise data stores such as SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, Synapse, or similar. * Cloud Experience: Exposure to cloud\-based data platforms (Azure preferred) and distributed data processing environments. * Business Intelligence Awareness: Basic understanding of reporting tools such as Power BI or Tableau, and how data pipelines support analytics and dashboards. * Industry Experience (Desirable): Experience within Financial Services or regulated environments is beneficial. If you are interested in this role or would like to discuss it further, please call Nidhi at \+353 1 645 5244 or email nidhi.tripathi@experis.ie.

title: Digital Brand & Communications Associate
description: **Digital Brand \& Communications Associate — Simply Voice** Get €10,000 of Executive Voice Coaching — In Exchange for less than 10 Hours a Week **Commitment: 10 Hours Per Week \| Duration: 6 Months \| Location: Hybrid/Remote** **⚠️ This is an unpaid internship.** In return you will receive 6 months of executive voice and leadership coaching with Simply Voice — the same coaching we deliver to CEOs at Amazon, Deloitte and Google. **Value: €10,000 plus.** *If you are looking for a paid role this is not the right opportunity for you.* However *,* Simply Voice is a growing business working at the highest corporate level. For the **right candidate** this residency has the potential to lead to a paid role. *This residency is built on a simple belief — that the best partnerships are ones where both sides grow. We will invest in you completely. You can come to every workshop and event that Simply Voice hosts throughout the year totally free of charge. We will coach you to become the most powerful, confident and compelling communicator you have ever been — the kind of speaker who commands any room, online or in person. In return we ask you to bring that same energy and commitment to helping Simply Voice grow — amplifying our voice, building our presence and helping us reach the leaders and organisations who need us most.* *We help you find your voice. You help us share ours.* *That is the Simply Voice Residency.* **We Are Here to Add, Not to Take** At Simply Voice, we believe presence is the ultimate currency. This residency is not a traditional internship — it is a high\-value, value\-for\-value exchange. We work with CEOs, senior leaders and organisations including Amazon, Deloitte, Google and EY across Ireland, the UK and Europe. We are looking for one dedicated individual — whether a hungry graduate, someone with a few years experience, or someone returning to the workforce — who wants to help us grow our digital presence while undergoing a personal transformation that will set them up for life. **A €10,000 Investment in You** This role is unpaid. In return you will receive a 6 month bespoke Executive Voice and Leadership Coaching programme worth €10,000 plus. We will coach you to master your vocal power, command any room and communicate with the calm authority that usually takes decades to build. You will leave this residency sounding — and feeling — completely different. **What You Will Actually Do** This is a digital marketing and brand role. Here is exactly what you will own: **Social Media Management — Day One Priority** * Managing and growing our LinkedIn and Instagram presence daily * Creating and scheduling content using Buffer * Building our Facebook presence and running Facebook ad campaigns * Keeping our social channels active, consistent and premium **Email Marketing** * Setting up and managing our email marketing and mailouts * Building and maintaining our audience list * Writing and scheduling regular email campaigns **SEO \& Google** * Basic SEO work across the Simply Voice website * Managing and optimising our Google My Business profile * Keyword research and implementing improvements **PR \& Media — Desirable But Not Essential** * Pitching Simply Voice to national radio — Newstalk, RTÉ * Targeting business podcasts and press opportunities * Writing press releases and media pitches **Who You Are** You have a background or strong interest in digital marketing, social media, communications or PR. Specifically you are comfortable with: * Instagram, LinkedIn and Facebook — not just using them but growing them * Facebook Ads Manager — you know how to set up and run a campaign * Canva and CapCut for content creation * Email marketing tools — Mailchimp, Flodesk or similar * Basic SEO principles and Google My Business * Buffer or similar scheduling tools **You are also:** * Self directed — you do not need constant hand holding * Organised — you deliver what you say you will deliver * Proactive — you spot opportunities and act on them * Trend aware — you know what is working on social media right now and you love it * Professional — you understand that Simply Voice works at the highest corporate level and everything you produce must reflect that **This is NOT the right role if you:** * Need constant supervision and direction * Are primarily interested in design or creative work * Cannot commit consistently each week * Are looking for a passive portfolio piece **A €10,000 Investment in You** For the right person this is genuinely life changing. Six months of executive voice and presence coaching — the same coaching we deliver to CEOs at Amazon, Deloitte and Google — is yours. You will leave with a voice, a presence and a confidence that will open doors for the rest of your career. **How to Apply** Generic CVs will not be considered. We want to hear your voice before we see your history. We have been flooded with AI generated cover letters. The irony is not lost on us — we are an executive voice coaching company. If you are using ChatGPT to tell us why you want to work with us, you have already missed the point. We are looking for the 1% who still know how to be human. Do not just click a button. Do not send a generic template. Pick up the phone. Send a voice note. Write a letter that is so authentically you that a computer could not have dreamt it up. Tell us: * Why you want to spend 6 months mastering your voice * How you would grow Simply Voice's digital presence from day one * What you would do in the first week to get the June event sold out Email **info@simplyvoice.ie, contact number \+353 876974332** with the subject **"The 2026 Simply Voice Residency"** **For more information \-** https://www.simplyvoice.ie/voiceresidency Closing date: **Friday 20th May 2026 at 5pm**

title: Digital Marketing Executive
description: **Digital Marketing Executive** Join a dynamic marketing team in Beacon Hospital, Sandyford, South Dublin. It's one of Europe's most innovative and technologically advanced private hospitals with over 2,000 staff working together as a team to deliver exceptional patient care. Help us to creatively communicate to patients, GPs, staff and the general public about all the new and innovative healthcare services we have to offer. The Digital Marketing Executive will report to the Marketing Director and will work closely with the Marketing Manager to develop and plan the digital marketing strategy for the hospital to drive online growth and brand awareness. The main objective of their role will be to plan, execute and manage all digital marketing including website content, SEO, SEM, email marketing and social media. The ideal candidate will have a speciality in social media, photography, videography, design, digital data analytics, have strong writing skills and be highly organised. They will also have the opportunity to work on other marketing activities to support the team and will gain experience in all aspects of marketing. **Job Description** * **Strategy Development:** Design and implement comprehensive digital marketing strategies to increase brand awareness, promote services and drive website engagement and traffic. * **Campaign Management:** Plan and execute SEO/SEM, email marketing, social media, and display advertising campaigns. * **Analytics and Reporting:** Monitor, analyse, and report on campaign performance (ROI and KPIs) to optimise results. * ***Content and Social Media*** \- Managing all our social media platforms, generating new content that is relevant, engaging and informative. Driving increased engagement and followers on all our social platforms. * ***Website*** \- ownership of the hospital's main website ensuring that all content is accurate, displayed correctly and up to date. Writing new content and refreshing existing content as well increasing * ***Photography and Videography*** \- taking photos and videos for use in hospital related content across all hospital platforms. * ***Staff Digital Comms*** \- writing content and producing our staff comms update emails. * ***Providing Support \& Assistance*** \- providing support and assistance to the team in relation to Events, Sponsorships, Design \& Print, Advertising Campaigns (Medical and Consumer) and in any other projects which the Department is tasked with. Assisting with the training and support of new junior members of the team. * ***Budget*** \- working closely with the rest of the marketing team to manage the marketing budget to ensure that all marketing activity and purchase orders are within budget. * ***Digital Initiatives*** \- Development and ownership of a new Beacon Hospital health related digital projects. * ***Creativity*** \- bringing new and creative ideas to a team that is always open to trying new and exciting marketing methods. **Skill Set** * Qualification in Digital Marketing * Experienced in social media and digital marketing * Strength at website management and development * Confident in data analytics * Skilled at photography, videography and graphic design * Ability to take ownership of projects and take them to completion * Ability to work off own initiative * Time management and prioritisation should not be a problem * Creative and a real passion for marketing * Strong writing skills and attention to detail * Confident, self\-starter with the ability to engage with people on all levels * Excellent organisational skills and advanced in excel, word and PowerPoint * Experience in using the below tools: **Common Tools used** Canva Constant Contact Meta \& Linkedin Platforms Email Marketing Platforms Adobe Suite WordPress Hootsuite \& Buffer Google Analytics/GA4 This marketing role will give you an incredible opportunity to grow and advance your skillset. You will own all Digital Marketing projects and be involved in supporting other areas of marketing. **Beacon Hospital is expanding, so come join our growing team!**

title: Editor Intern
description: **Editor Intern (Unpaid) at Babylon!** Are you passionate about storytelling, content creation, and refining written material? Do you want to gain hands\-on editorial experience in a multicultural media platform? Join Babylon as an **Editor Intern** and help shape content that informs, inspires, and connects communities! **About Babylon :** Babylon Radio is a digital platform providing news, entertainment, and resources for the international community in Ireland. Our mission is to celebrate cultures, make connections, and share meaningful stories that matter. **About the Role:** As an Editor Intern, you’ll be responsible for reviewing, refining, and enhancing content across our website, blogs, and social media. This is an excellent opportunity to gain real\-world editorial experience, develop your writing and editing skills, and contribute to a growing media platform. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Content Editing:** * Review and edit articles, blog posts, and multimedia content for clarity, coherence, and accuracy. * Ensure adherence to Babylon Radio’s editorial style and tone of voice. * **Writing \& Content Development:** * Assist in creating high\-quality content, including news stories, features, and opinion pieces. * Generate ideas and provide input for content strategy. * **Fact\-Checking \& Quality Control:** * Verify sources and ensure published content maintains journalistic integrity. * Ensure all published material is free of grammatical, factual, and structural errors. * **Collaboration:** * Work closely with writers, journalists, and social media teams to optimize content. * Provide constructive feedback to content creators to improve writing quality. * **SEO \& Audience Engagement:** * Implement SEO best practices to increase content visibility. * Assist in structuring content for better readability and engagement. **What You Will Gain:** * **Real\-World Experience:** Work in a professional editorial environment and build your portfolio. * **Skill Development:** Enhance your editing, writing, and content strategy skills. * **Networking Opportunities:** Connect with writers, journalists, and creatives in a multicultural setting. **Who We’re Looking For:** * **Skills:** * Strong editing and proofreading skills with attention to detail. * Excellent command of the English language. * Familiarity with SEO best practices is a plus. * Experience with WordPress or other content management systems is advantageous. * **Attributes:** * Passionate about storytelling, multiculturalism, and digital media. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. * Proactive, self\-motivated, and open to constructive feedback. * **Availability:** Able to commit to a 3\-6 month internship. **Internship Details:** * **Duration:** 3\-6 months (flexible based on your availability). * **Location:** Hybrid (based in Dublin, Ireland). **How to Apply:** If you’re ready to sharpen your editorial skills and contribute to impactful content, we’d love to hear from you! Please send your CV, a writing sample, and a brief cover letter explaining why you’re the perfect fit for this role to **info@babylonradio.com** . **Join Babylon Radio and help us craft compelling stories that inspire and inform!**

title: Electrical Designer Coordinator
description: **Summary** We’re seeking an experienced Electrical BIM professional to lead the delivery of electrical models for a major pharmaceutical manufacturing facility. This role is ideal for someone confident in technical ownership and coordination across multiple disciplines, ensuring accuracy, constructability, and compliance throughout the project lifecycle. **Location and Working Pattern** Location: Dublin Working Pattern: \[Remote / Hybrid / On\-site – to be confirmed] **Responsibilities** * Lead the development, management, and governance of electrical BIM models in Revit in line with project BIM Execution Plans, client standards, and industry best practice * Model complex electrical systems including containment, power distribution, equipment layouts, earthing, and lighting for pharmaceutical facilities * Deliver fully coordinated layouts and construction\-ready electrical drawings supporting all project phases * Ensure model accuracy, robustness, audit readiness, and compliance with data management requirements * Coordinate electrical models with mechanical, HVAC, process, architectural, automation, and structural disciplines in a federated BIM environment * Execute and manage clash detection and resolution using Navisworks or similar tools * Interpret electrical schematics, SLDs, layouts, and vendor documentation into accurate BIM outputs * Act as BIM interface for electrical engineering, liaising with teams, vendors, and contractors * Mentor junior BIM modellers, ensuring best practice and promoting workflow improvements **Must\-have Requirements** \[Essential qualifications, BIM/Revit/NAVISWORKS experience – insert details when confirmed] **Nice\-to\-have Skills** \[Any additional tools, sector experience, or certifications – insert details when confirmed] **Apply Now** If you have the experience to lead electrical BIM delivery for complex projects and want to make an impact, get in touch with us today to apply.

title: Electrical Quality Inspector
description: Electrical Quality Inspector, Kildare, Ireland Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. Responsibilities Of The Role** * Quality Management System (QMS) Oversight * Implement and maintain the Mercury QMS in compliance with Mercury SOPs. * Assist in the development of any project specific QA work procedures. * Assist in the development of any project specific ITPs. * Hold Quality Induction Workshops to promote awareness of the project specific requirements. * Work closely with engineering, operations, and project management teams. * Inspection \& Compliance * Conduct and record surveillance / in progress inspections of all work carried out in on the project. * Ensure compliance with industry standards (e.g., IEC, IEEE), customer specifications, and regulatory requirements. * Conduct audits and inspections of electrical products and systems * Documentation \& Reporting * Prepare and verify quality documentation, including inspection reports and certifications. * Conduct and record final inspections on all work carried out on the project. * Continuous Improvement * Develop and monitor KPIs to drive quality improvements. * Lead root cause analysis and corrective actions for defects and failures. * Apply Lean, Six Sigma, and 5S methodologies to optimize processes. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Strong knowledge of electrical systems and standards. * Proficiency in Quality Management Systems (QMS) and KPI development. * Experience with internal audits, corrective/preventive actions, and compliance documentation. * Familiarity with calibration systems, test plans, and quality documentation. **Desirable Criteria** * Attention to detail – critical for identifying defects and ensuring compliance. * Effective communication – to liaise with production, suppliers, and regulatory bodies. * Problem\-solving – proactive in identifying root causes and implementing solutions. * Adaptability \& continuous improvement mindset – driving innovation in QA processes. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Apply Now

title: Enterprise Business Development Representative
description: We’re looking to hire an Enterprise Business Development Representative (BDR) who is responsible for generating qualified pipeline for the sales organisation by identifying, engaging, and nurturing potential customers. This role focuses on outbound prospecting, inbound lead qualification, and creating meaningful conversations with key decision‑makers in target accounts. **About Sidetrade** Sidetrade is an AI company, listed on Euronext Growth, on a mission to revolutionize the way enterprises unlock value from their customers leveraging its Order\-to\-Cash Intelligence platform and its Data Lake. We’re proud of our 38 nationalities and these diverse perspectives drive our innovation, one team culture and a customer\-first mindset. Sudetrade is positioned as a **Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ Leader** since 2022\. We value passion over perfection. So, if you’re eager to learn and bring great energy, we want to hear from you. Be you. Grow with us. Curious about Sidetrade? Catch the **Sidetrade Inside Out podcast**. **Requirements** **What you’ll be doing:** * Conduct outbound prospecting through email, phone, social channels, and sequences to target ideal customer profiles (ICPs). * Qualify inbound leads and follow up promptly to assess needs, buying intent, and fit. * Build and manage a healthy pipeline of early‑stage opportunities for Account Executives (AEs) or Regional Sales Managers (RSMs) * Engage prospects to understand their challenges, pain points, and objectives. * Deliver compelling value propositions tailored to the buyer persona (e.g., CTO, CMO, CFO, Operations). * Schedule high‑quality discovery meetings or demos for the sales team. * Partner with AEs/RSMs to develop account strategies and outreach plans. * Provide feedback to Marketing on lead quality, campaign performance, and customer insights. * Maintain strong CRM hygiene with accurate and timely activity tracking. * Achieve monthly and quarterly KPIs, including meetings booked, qualified opportunities created, and outreach activity goals. * Track key metrics such as conversion rates, sales cycle inputs, and pipeline contribution. * Continuously refine messaging and outreach tactics based on performance data. **What you’ll bring**: * 1–3 years of experience in sales, BDR/SDR roles, lead generation, or customer‑facing roles (preferably in SaaS or technology). * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Tenacity and resilience in handling outbound prospecting. * Curiosity and ability to ask insightful questions. * Familiarity with modern sales tech stacks (Salesforce/HubSpot, Outreach, SalesLoft, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, etc.). * Ability to quickly grasp technical products and translate value to prospects. * Organised, self\-motivated, and comfortable working in a KPI‑driven environment. * Experience with outbound prospecting preferred. * Demonstrated ability to meet or exceed activity and pipeline targets. * Bachelor’s degree desirable but not required. * Ability to speak English fluently. **Benefits** * Attractive location\- office based in the heart of Dublin city centre. * Hybrid work model – a flexible mix of in\-office and remote days. * Great culture – active Social Club organizing regular team events and activities. * Health \& wellness – medical coverage, life insurance, and other wellness programs. * Time off – competitive paid holidays plus public holidays. * Career growth \& compensation – competitive salary, equal opportunities, learning \& mentorship programs, and advancement support. Because when you thrive, we all succeed! We’re committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive experience for every candidate. If you need any accommodation during the hiring process, just let us know. **Agencies** We only accept applications from invited agencies via our Workable portal. Unsolicited CVs sent to managers or HR won’t be subject to fees.
